title: Recognising AML with CBFB::MYH11 (inv(16)/t(16;16)) from integrated laboratory findings
learning_objectives: Recognise the morphology and immunophenotype of CBFB::MYH11 AML
Understand diagnostic versus prognostic significance of accompanying mutations
clinical_vignette: A 41-year-old woman presents with fatigue, easy bruising and intermittent abdominal discomfort. Peripheral blood shows circulating blasts and eosinophilia. Bone marrow examination and rapid FISH are performed.

morphology.peripheral_blood: Myeloblasts with occasional Auer rods are present.
morphology.bone_marrow: Hypercellular marrow with myelomonocytic blasts and abnormal eosinophils containing immature basophilic granules.

flow_cytometry.interpretation: Immunophenotype supports AML with myelomonocytic differentiation and is characteristic of CBFB::MYH11-associated AML.

cytogenetics: FISH detects CBFB::MYH11 fusion. Conventional karyotype confirms inv(16)(p13.1q22).
molecular_genetics: KIT mutation testing pending. FLT3-ITD negative.
integrated_laboratory_diagnosis: AML with CBFB::MYH11 fusion (core-binding factor AML).
question: Which feature is the strongest clue suggesting CBFB::MYH11 AML before cytogenetic confirmation?
options: Abnormal marrow eosinophils with immature basophilic granules and myelomonocytic differentiation
correct_answer: A. Abnormal marrow eosinophils with immature basophilic granules and myelomonocytic differentiation
explanation: AML with CBFB::MYH11 typically demonstrates myelomonocytic differentiation together with abnormal eosinophils containing immature basophilic granules. Flow cytometry often shows monocytic markers with aberrant CD2. Identification of the CBFB::MYH11 fusion confirms the diagnosis under WHO 5th Edition and ICC 2022.

why_other_options_are_wrong.B: Fibrosis is not a defining feature of CBFB::MYH11 AML.
why_other_options_are_wrong.C: Aberrant CD19 is more characteristic of RUNX1::RUNX1T1 AML.
why_other_options_are_wrong.D: MPO is usually positive in this subtype.
why_other_options_are_wrong.E: Erythroid hyperplasia is not a defining morphologic clue.

frcpath_pearl: When abnormal eosinophils accompany myelomonocytic AML, actively consider inv(16)/t(16;16) and request rapid CBFB::MYH11 testing.
take_home_message: Morphology, flow cytometry and rapid molecular/cytogenetic testing should be integrated to rapidly identify core-binding factor AML.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th edition
